Title: RpoN1 and RpoN2 play different regulatory roles in virulence traits, flagellar biosynthesis, and basal metabolism in Xanthomonas campestris

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RNA polymerase sigma-54 factor (RpoN); sigma-54/RpoN family with RNAP core-binding domain and RpoN domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation of basal metabolism genes; likely indirect regulation for fatty acid and quorum-sensing metabolite changes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: rpoN1 deletion altered expression of basal metabolism genes including leuA and rpfB; direction for individual direct targets not firmly established
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: branched-chain fatty acid synthesis and diffusible signal factor family signal production
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: branched-chain fatty acids; DSF family signals (DSF, BDSF, IDSF)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: fatty acid; quorum-sensing fatty acid signal
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes branched-chain fatty acid accumulation and restrains overproduction of DSF family quorum-sensing signals, especially DSF
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of rpoN1 significantly decreased branched-chain fatty acids, especially iso-C15:0 and anteiso-C15:0, and increased n-C15:0. 2. In an rpfC mutant background, deletion of rpoN1 significantly increased DSF family signals, especially DSF. 3. RNA-seq showed that rpoN1 broadly affected basal metabolism genes, including leuA and rpfB, indicating specialization in metabolic regulation distinct from rpoN2.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-frame knockout; double mutant analysis; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; HPLC quantification of DSF family signals; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent metabolic regulator specialized for basal metabolism, particularly BCFA and DSF-family signal balance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with RNA polymerase holoenzyme as sigma-54 factor
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in NYG medium at 28°C for fatty acid analysis; DSF measured after 36 h liquid culture, largely in ΔrpfC background
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not measurably affect virulence, motility, biofilm, or EPS under tested conditions, but altered membrane-related fatty acid composition and quorum-sensing metabolite output
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: BCFAs contribute to membrane/lipid metabolism; DSF-family signals mediate quorum sensing and bacterial physiological regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cellular lipid fraction for BCFAs; culture supernatant for DSF/BDSF/IDSF
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; accession NA (data available from corresponding author upon request)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RpoN1 and RpoN2 were not functionally interchangeable. Mechanistic link to BCFA/DSF metabolism is inferred from transcriptome and metabolite profiling; direct promoter binding to metabolic genes was not shown.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RNA polymerase sigma-54 factor (RpoN); sigma-54/RpoN family with RNAP core-binding domain and RpoN domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates fliC and fliQ transcription by binding their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flagellar biosynthesis; indirectly affects EPS production and biofilm formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes EPS production and biofilm formation; required for full virulence, swimming motility, and flagellar biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of rpoN2 strongly reduced virulence and in planta bacterial growth in cabbage. 2. rpoN2 mutants lost the single polar flagellum and swimming motility. 3. EMSA, RT-qPCR, RNA-seq, and lacZ reporter assays showed that RpoN2 directly binds the fliC and fliQ promoters and positively regulates these flagellar genes. 4. rpoN2 deletion also reduced EPS production and biofilm formation, likely contributing to virulence defects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-frame knockout; complementation; plant virulence assay; in planta bacterial counts; RNA-seq; TEM; swimming assay; EMSA; RT-qPCR; promoter-lacZ reporter; EPS quantification; crystal violet biofilm assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator for flagellar gene expression and major determinant of EPS-associated virulence traits
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with RNA polymerase holoenzyme as sigma-54 factor
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in NYG medium at 28°C; virulence tested on Brassica oleracea 'Jingfeng No. 1' by leaf clipping
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters of fliC and fliQ; fliC-lacZ transcriptional fusion reporter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of rpoN2 causes severe defects in motility, flagellar assembly, biofilm and EPS production, and virulence
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: EPS supports biofilm matrix formation, host colonization, and pathogenicity
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; accession NA (data available from corresponding author upon request)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although homologous to RpoN1, RpoN2 has distinct non-interchangeable function centered on flagellar regulation and virulence. The direct metabolic readout is EPS reduction; flagellar phenotypes are the main experimentally validated downstream outputs.
